_note: Slab c.1000-880 BC — three HISTORICAL threads splicing onto existing nodes as new families (NEVER re-parenting an existing king). THREAD 1 EGYPT 22ND (LIBYAN / BUBASTITE) DYNASTY (EG_): a fresh family hung off the existing bridge EG_THIRDINTERMEDIATE (referenced as a string; it already parents EG_PIYE in 10b and EG_SMENDES1 in 20b, so this is a parallel branch, not a re-parenting). Succession spine with '—' consorts: Shoshenq I (c.943, the founder, the biblical SHISHAK who invaded Judah & looted Solomon's Temple ~925 BC) -> Osorkon I -> Takelot I -> Osorkon II (c.872) -> Shoshenq III. THE 21ST<->22ND DYNASTIC MARRIAGE (the priority cross-line, within Egypt): Maatkare B is drawn ONCE as a DAUGHTER of the existing 21st-Dynasty king EG_PSUSENNES2 (referenced by id, NOT re-parented — it is a leaf in 20b), then married to Osorkon I; their son the High Priest Shoshenq Q ties the Tanite 21st house into the Bubastite 22nd. Shoshenq I's OTHER attested marriage — to the secondary Libyan queen Patareshnes (Penreshnes), daughter of a Libyan chief — is drawn as its own family producing their son Nimlot B (commander of infantry at Herakleopolis); accurately a Libyan, not a royal-line, tie. THREAD 2 NEO-ASSYRIAN REVIVAL (AS_): the recovery kings Ashur-dan II (c.934), Adad-nirari II (c.911) & Ashurnasirpal II (c.883) ALREADY EXIST in 10c_assyria.json and are NOT recreated or re-parented. The one missing link, Tukulti-Ninurta II (c.890), is added as a NEW child of the existing AS_ADADNIRARI2 — Adad-nirari II already parents Ashurnasirpal II there, so this second family is a parallel branch (no double-parenting of any existing king); Tukulti-Ninurta II's own son Ashurnasirpal II is noted in-label, not by a second parent-edge. THREAD 3 PHOENICIA / TYRE (PHO_): extends the existing PHO_HIRAM1 (referenced by id, NOT re-parented) down the Josephus/Menander king-list — Baal-Eser I (son of Hiram) -> Abdastartus (son) -> a bridge compressing the usurper interlude (the four sons of Abdastartus's nurse: Astartus, Deleastartus, Astharymus, Phelles) -> Ithobaal I (Ethbaal, c.887), a priest of Astarte who murdered Phelles & seized the throne (FLAGGED usurpation, NOT descent), king of Tyre & Sidon. His daughter Jezebel is created as a leaf so a future slab can marry her to King Ahab of Israel (that cross-line marriage belongs to the later slab). Prefixes: Egypt=EG_, Assyria=AS_, Phoenicia=PHO_. Every child/accession year is SMALLER than its parent's chain year. Dates are conventional Third-Intermediate / Neo-Assyrian / Tyrian-king-list figures and are debated (flagged). Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia 'Shoshenq I', 'Shishak', 'Osorkon I', 'Maatkare B', 'Patareshnes', 'Twenty-second Dynasty of Egypt', 'Takelot I', 'Osorkon II', 'Shoshenq III', 'Tukulti-Ninurta II', 'Ithobaal I', plus the Biblical Archaeology Society 'The House of Tyre' and Josephus/Menander of Ephesus. No fabrication; every uncertain or non-descent link flagged in-node.

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (1 of 6). EG_THIRDINTERMEDIATE (existing bridge, referenced by id, NOT re-parented — it already parents EG_PIYE and EG_SMENDES1) & a '—' consort; the founder Shoshenq I as child. He is the biblical Shishak.

children.label:
👑 Shoshenq I (Hedjkheperre)
c.943 BC · founder of the Libyan / Bubastite 22nd Dynasty · a Meshwesh chief of Libyan descent who reunified Egypt from Tanis & Bubastis · identified with the biblical SHISHAK who invaded Judah in Rehoboam's 5th year (~925 BC) & carried off the treasures of Solomon's Temple (1 Kings 14; 2 Chron 12); his Karnak Bubastite Portal relief lists the conquered towns · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (2 of 6) — Shoshenq I's secondary marriage. EG_SHOSHENQ1 & Patareshnes (Penreshnes), a secondary queen & daughter of an unnamed Libyan chief — an accurate LIBYAN tie, not a royal-line one; their son Nimlot B as child. (The tie into the old 21st-Dynasty royal line came a generation later, through Osorkon I's marriage to Maatkare — see family 5.)

b.label:
Patareshnes (Penreshnes)
c.940 BC · secondary queen consort of Shoshenq I · daughter of an unnamed Libyan chief; mother of Nimlot B · historical

children.label:
Nimlot B
c.915 BC · son of Shoshenq I & Patareshnes · appointed by his father as Commander of all the infantry at Herakleopolis Magna, a key military governorship of the 22nd Dynasty · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (3 of 6) — succession spine. EG_SHOSHENQ1 & a '—' consort (his chief queen Karomat A); his son & successor Osorkon I as child.

children.label:
👑 Osorkon I (Sekhemkheperre)
c.922 BC · 22nd Dynasty · son of Shoshenq I (by the chief queen Karomat A); a long, prosperous reign that endowed the temples with vast quantities of gold & silver · historical

families.note: EGYPT 21ST-DYNASTY DAUGHTER — drawn ONCE. EG_PSUSENNES2 (existing last king of the 21st Dynasty, referenced by id, NOT re-parented — a leaf in 20b) & a '—' consort; his daughter Maatkare B as child. She marries into the 22nd Dynasty in the next family.

children.label:
Maatkare B
c.945 BC · king's daughter of Psusennes II, LAST king of the 21st Dynasty · her marriage to Osorkon I of the 22nd Dynasty knit the outgoing Tanite royal house into the incoming Libyan line · historical

families.note: THE 21ST<->22ND DYNASTIC MARRIAGE — the priority cross-line union (cross-DYNASTY, within Egypt). EG_OSORKON1 (22nd Dynasty) ● EG_MAATKARE (daughter of the 21st-Dynasty king Psusennes II); their son the High Priest of Amun Shoshenq Q ties the two royal houses together. Maatkare is reused here from family 4 (drawn once, no re-parenting).

children.label:
👑 Shoshenq Q (High Priest of Amun)
c.910 BC · son of Osorkon I & Maatkare B · High Priest of Amun at Thebes; through his mother a grandson of Psusennes II, embodying the merger of the 21st & 22nd royal lines · often identified with the ephemeral king Heqakheperre Shoshenq II of the Tanis silver-coffin burial (identification debated) · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (4 of 6) — succession spine. EG_OSORKON1 & a '—' consort (queen Tashedkhons); his son & successor Takelot I as child. (Osorkon I is reused as a parent here; drawn once as a child in family 3.)

children.label:
👑 Takelot I (Hedjkheperre)
c.885 BC · 22nd Dynasty · son of Osorkon I by queen Tashedkhons; a poorly documented reign, overshadowed at Thebes by the growing independence of the Amun priesthood · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (5 of 6). EG_TAKELOT1 & a '—' consort (queen Kapes); his son & successor Osorkon II as child.

children.label:
👑 Osorkon II (Usermaatre)
c.872 BC · 22nd Dynasty · son of Takelot I by queen Kapes · a long reign; his forces joined the Levantine coalition that fought Shalmaneser III of Assyria at Qarqar (853 BC); great builder at Bubastis · historical

families.note: EGYPT 22ND DYNASTY (6 of 6) — close of this slab. EG_OSORKON2 & a '—' consort; Shoshenq III as successor. FLAGGED: Shoshenq III succeeded Osorkon II but his exact filiation is debated — he may not have been a direct son (succession, not certain descent).

children.label:
👑 Shoshenq III
c.837 BC · 22nd Dynasty · succeeded Osorkon II; his exact parentage is DEBATED — possibly not a direct son (succession, flagged) · during his ~39-yr reign the dynasty fractured as a rival 23rd Dynasty emerged at Thebes/Leontopolis · historical

families.note: NEO-ASSYRIAN REVIVAL — the one missing link. AS_ADADNIRARI2 (existing Neo-Assyrian king in 10c, referenced by id, NOT re-parented — it already parents AS_ASHURNASIRPAL2) & a '—' consort; his son Tukulti-Ninurta II as child. Ashur-dan II & Adad-nirari II & Ashurnasirpal II already exist and are NOT recreated. Tukulti-Ninurta II's own son Ashurnasirpal II is noted in-label (drawn in the existing chain), so no existing king is double-parented.

children.label:
👑 Tukulti-Ninurta II
c.890 BC · Neo-Assyria · son of Adad-nirari II & FATHER of Ashurnasirpal II (who is drawn in the existing succession chain) · consolidated the recovery, campaigning in the Zagros & along the middle Euphrates and leaving detailed annals · historical

families.note: PHOENICIA / TYRE (1 of 5). PHO_HIRAM1 (existing king of Tyre, referenced by id, NOT re-parented) & a '—' consort; his son & successor Baal-Eser I as child (Josephus/Menander king-list).

children.label:
👑 Baal-Eser I (Beleazarus)
c.945 BC · King of Tyre · son of Hiram I · known from the Tyrian king-list preserved by Josephus from Menander of Ephesus · historical

families.note: PHOENICIA / TYRE (2 of 5). PHO_BAALESER1 & a '—' consort; his son & successor Abdastartus as child. Abdastartus was assassinated by the four sons of his nurse, opening the usurper interlude compressed in the next node.

children.label:
👑 Abdastartus (Abd-Astart)
c.929 BC · King of Tyre · son of Baal-Eser I & grandson of Hiram I · assassinated after ~9 years by the four sons of his nurse, who then usurped the throne in succession · historical

families.note: PHOENICIA / TYRE (3 of 5) — bridge node compressing the usurper interlude (no crown). PHO_ABDASTARTUS & a '—' consort; the bridge as child. It stands for the four sons of Abdastartus's nurse who reigned in succession — Astartus, Deleastartus, Astharymus (Aserymus) & Phelles — before Ithobaal I seized power.

children.label:
Tyre usurper interlude
c.909–888 BC · the four sons of Abdastartus's nurse — Astartus, Deleastartus, Astharymus (Aserymus) & Phelles — ruled Tyre in succession after murdering Abdastartus, until the priest Ithobaal killed the last of them, Phelles · historical (bridge)

families.note: PHOENICIA / TYRE (4 of 5) — the father of Jezebel. PHO_TYREUSURPERS bridge & a '—' consort; Ithobaal I as successor. FLAGGED usurpation, NOT descent: Ithobaal was a priest of Astarte who murdered Phelles & seized the throne, uniting Tyre & Sidon.

children.label:
👑 Ithobaal I (Ethbaal)
c.887 BC · King of Tyre & Sidon · a priest of Astarte who murdered King Phelles & seized the throne (usurpation, NOT descent from the Hiram line) · reigned ~32 yr, expanding Tyre's power over all Phoenicia & founding colonies · the biblical Ethbaal (1 Kings 16:31), FATHER OF JEZEBEL · historical

families.note: PHOENICIA / TYRE (5 of 5) — the Jezebel link, made ready for a future slab. PHO_ITHOBAAL1 & a '—' consort; his daughter Jezebel as child (leaf). Her marriage to King Ahab of Israel is a CROSS-LINE marriage that belongs to a LATER slab (Ahab is not yet in the tree here); she is created now so that union can be drawn later.

children.label:
Jezebel
c.870 BC · Phoenician princess of Tyre · daughter of Ithobaal I · WILL marry King Ahab of Israel (a future-slab cross-line marriage between the Tyrian & Israelite houses); brought the cult of Baal-Melqart to Samaria, becoming the archetypal antagonist of the prophet Elijah · historical
